about.description: My background, my values and why I do what I do. Not corporate bio, just me and my passion for product design.
about.keywords: about Adriel Zimbril, Product Designer SaaS, SaaS trainer, SaaS product design journey, ShiroSaaS method

craft-section.description: Putting the user at the center of every decision, guiding and teaching creators to transform every idea into unforgettable experiences.
craft-section.title: Transform ideas into products that count.

header-section.description: Since my beginnings in design, I've learned that the most successful product isn't the one you see first, but the one you understand, test and evolve with intention.
header-section.description-secondary:
Sic itur ad astra — that's how we reach the stars .

Since I was small, I've always been fascinated by technology and invention. Iron Man gave me a taste for possibility: I remember my eyes sparkling when I saw his armor and the inspiration that crossed me at each episode. I spent hours imagining, tinkering, and dreaming of creating my own robots.

By giving free rein to this passion I discovered design and with time, I understood that my dream wasn't limited to building machines: I wanted to create experiences that touch people, that solve real problems and that give the desire to be used. Design became my playground, my lab to imagine, build and transform raw ideas into concrete products and design experiences that count.

Today, I transform ideas into concrete experiences, helping founders, teams and creators to clarify their problems, imagine solutions and launch quickly. My approach combines thoughtful design, agile strategy and continuous learning so that every project has a real impact.

I like to question what exists, explore what works and what doesn't, and build in a purposeful way. I prefer to do things with a why rather than by habit.

In parallel, I share the methods and resources from my creative experiences so that others can learn to design and launch products themselves, with confidence.

For me, design is not a standalone deliverable: it is a collaborative, creative and human process.
header-section.title:
Hello, I am  
 Adriel Zimbril  
 Product Designer & Trainer
header-section.title-secondary:
Create, simplify, inspire  
 Welcome to my universe

content.conclusion: Facts, numbers, painful failures and unexpected victories. Design as it really happens ❤️.
content.item1: What works (and what doesn’t) in the interfaces I create
content.item2: User feedback that made me change my mind

content.title: In this newsletter I simply share what I learn.

cta.description: A guide on UX mistakes I often notice in SaaS. Concrete observations I’ve gathered through my projects.
cta.note: And of course, you can unsubscribe whenever you want. I only write when I have something useful to share.
cta.title: I’ve prepared something to start with.

newsletterSignup.preview: Hi! I wanted to talk to you about something close to my heart...

story.intro: A few weeks ago, one of the clients of one of my apps wrote to tell me that it had taken a long time to undo an action. I realized it was due to a design choice I liked... but which complicated the experience.
story.philosophy: What I’m really interested in is understanding how people use what we create. Why they abandon at certain points, why they come back, what really helps them in their daily life.
story.presentation: My name is Adriel, and for 5 years I’ve been creating interfaces for SaaS and I noticed something: we often complicate things while users are simply looking for simplicity.

newsletterSignup.subject: Hello, it’s Adriel !
newsletterSignup.tagline: Problem Solver & Your SaaS Product designer 🦄

why.motivation: I want to share these small discoveries, these moments where you say 'oh, I wouldn’t have thought of that'. No pretension, just shared curiosity ❤️.
why.reason: Because I enjoy understanding how things work. And in design there is often a gap between what we think will work and what actually works.
why.title: Why do I really write ?

productDelivery.message1: I’m glad you’re part of this adventure. I hope this resource helps you go further, faster.
productDelivery.message2: Your feedback really helps me improve my content for future readers. And if you have questions along the way, don’t hesitate to ask!
productDelivery.preview: Your {productType} is ready to be downloaded !

code.feedback: When you have time to read it, I’d love to know what you think. Is it useful for your current projects? What questions remain?
code.tip: 🚀 Tip: try applying directly what you learn.

course.feedback: After following a few modules, I’d love to hear your feedback. Does the content meet your expectations? Are there points you’d like to see explored further ?
course.tip: 💡 Quick tip: take notes while you go through the modules, it helps to remember and apply.

ebook.feedback: When you have time to read it, I’d love to know what you think. Is it useful for your current projects? What questions remain?
ebook.tip: 📖 Suggestion: read it in small sections and try applying what you learn right away.

figma_template.feedback: When you have time to read it, I’d love to know what you think. Is it useful for your current projects? What questions remain?
figma_template.tip: 🚀 Tip: try applying directly what you learn.

masterclass.feedback: Once you have watched the masterclass, tell me what you think! Does it answer your questions? What stood out the most?
masterclass.tip: 🎥 Tip: watch it in a quiet place with a notebook nearby. The best ideas often come while listening.

video.feedback: Once you have watched the video, tell me what you think! Does it answer your questions? What stood out the most?
video.tip: 🎯 Tip: watch it in a quiet place with a notebook nearby. The best ideas often come while listening.
